semanticCore: Subtlety, kindness, gentleness, microscopic detail, or grace
quranicRelevance: God's attributes of absolute kindness and subtle awareness (Lateef - 'wa huwa al-lateefu al-khabeer'), and behaving with extreme gentleness ('wal-yatalattaf wa la yush'iranna').

keyDerivations.meaning: Subtle, kind, gracious, knowing the microscopic details, or gentle
To behave with subtlety, gentleness, caution, or soft action

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَلْيَتَلَطَّفْ وَلَا يُشْعِرَنَّ بِكُمْ أَحَدًا (18:19): And let him behave with extreme subtlety/gentleness and not let anyone become aware of you (cave sleepers' strategy).
discardedNoise: Modern air conditioning or room temperature adjustments.
Humor and jokes (Lateefah) in modern parlor talk.
summaryEn: The root denotes subtlety, bridging God's microscopic, gentle care (Lateef) with the sleepers' advice to act with silent gentleness (Yatalattaf) to avoid detection.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على اللطف والدقة، رابطاً بين اسم الله (اللطيف) المحيط بصغائر الأمور، وتوجيه أصحاب الكهف للسلوك الخفي الحذر (وليتلطّف).
rawText: أَلْطَفَهُ 4 He gave him a gift or present. (TA.) === He showed him kindness, or goodness, and affection and gentleness, and regard for his circumstances, بِكَذَا [by such a thing, or such an action, &c.]. (S, K, TA.) Often occurring in the latter sense: but أَلْطَفَهَ بِكَذَا, expl. in the S and K by بَرَّهُ بِهِ, may mean He presented him with such a thing; like وَصَلَهُ بِهِ. === See أَخْلَطَهُ. الإِلْطَافُ Self-pollution, by a woman: see جَلَدَ عُمَيْرَةَ in art. جلد. تَلَطَّفَ لِلْأَمْرِ 5 i. q. تَرَفَّقَ: (S:) see طَبَّ. === I. q. تَكَلَّفَ اللُّطْفَ. (Bd xviii. 18.) === تَلَطَّفَ بِهِ i. q. تَرَفَّقَ. (Mgh in art. رفق.) لَاطَفَ 3 He caressed; treated with blandishment; soothed; coaxed; wheedled; cajoled: i. q. بَارَّهُ. (S, K.) === لَاطَفَهُ also signifies He spoke softly, gently, or blandly, to him. (TA.) He acted in a good manner with him: (KL:) manifested goodness towards him: (PS:) he acted towards him with goodness: and he did so, experiencing from him the same: (TK:) or rather, as syn. with بَارَّهُ, he behaved towards him with goodness and affection and gentleness, and regard for his circumstances; or did so, experiencing from him the same behaviour. لَطَفٌ A gift, or present: pl. أَلْطَافٌ. (MA.) === See لَطَفةٌ. لَطَفَةٌ A present; i. e. a thing sent to another in token of courtesy or honour; syn. هَدِيَّةٌ; (S, K;) as also 🡻لَطَفٌ, as stated by Z and others: pl. of the latter أَلْطَافٌ. (TA.) لَطُفَ 1 It (a thing) was small, or little; (S, Msb, K, KL;) and slender, thin, or fine: (K, KL:) and elegant, or graceful. (KL.) لَطِيفٌ Gentle, gracious, courteous, or benignant: and also subtle; knowing with respect to the subtilties, niceties, abstrusities, or obscurities, of things, affairs, or cases: in both of these senses often applied to a man. And Refined in manners, &c. === Obscure, recondite, or abstruse, language. (Kull.) === See Ham, p. 455. === Applied to a medicine, &c., Delicate: see سَوْسَنٌ. لَطِيفَةٌ A nice, subtile, subtilely excogitated, quaint, facetious, or witty, saying, expression, or allusion; a witticism; a quaint conceit. === [A nicety of language;] any indication of subtile meaning, apparent to the understanding, but not to be expressed; as [matters of] the sciences of taste (عُلُوم الأَدْوَاق). (KT.) لَطَّفَ 2 It (a medicine) acted as an attenuant, and as an emollient. === لَطَّفَهُ, inf. n. تَلْطِيفٌ, [He made it slender]. (A, and K, art. حشر; &c.) لُطْفٌ Gentleness: graciousness; courtesy; civility: (S, &c.:) see رِفْقٌ: and delicacy of flavour, &c.
